label: Vesperglass System
description: Vesperglass System stands within the Hushlight Expanse’s void-basin lattice, where particulate radiance concentrates into a persistent, mirrorlike halo along the system’s heliopause. Its primary star burns with a muted, copper-white spectrum that attenuates rapidly, producing long twilight gradients that render distance unusually legible to deep-field instruments. A broad belt of vitrified debris occupies the middle orbits, its shards retaining faint charge and tracing stable arcs that align with the Expanse’s radiance currents. Navigation records treat the system as a fixed waypoint, defined less by brightness than by the orderly geometry of its hushlit reflections.
